text: P1 - Academic Document Field Classification and Extraction.
Updated Build Plan for CONSTANT, CONTROLLED VARIABLE, and OPEN VARIABLE
Prepared for Academic Document Intelligence Project.
P1 is an academic document field-classification and extraction system. Given a scanned or digital university document,.
the system identifies which visible content is fixed template text and which content represents document-specific fields.
.One institution, for example JNTUH or a single selected university..
: 50-100 real documents collected and annotated for the first sprint, expanding to 100-300 documents after the
The system must classify and extract fields such as:.
. Seals, signatures, logos, and other structural regions..
The output should be a versioned JSON object that can be reviewed, audited, and used by downstream verification
Do not start with deep learning only. The first useful system should be a template-aware, OCR-based baseline..
2. Normalize layouts into a shared coordinate system.
3. Cluster OCR boxes by position and text similarity.
4. Identify repeated text as template content..
5. Identify non-repeated values as candidate fields..
6. Use this baseline as both an MVP and a weak-label generator for later model training..
This gives a working system quickly and prevents the project from depending entirely on a large model that may not
The original binary taxonomy, CONSTANT vs VARIABLE, is not enough for real academic documents. Many
important fields change across documents but come from a controlled set of possible values. Therefore, the final
taxonomy should use three primary field classes..
Additional labels are useful for region-level understanding:.
A field such as Bachelor of Technology should not be treated the same way as a student name. It changes across
documents, but it belongs to a known list of valid degree names..
This reduces annotator disagreement and allows automatic correction or review using lookup lists..
English first, but benchmark Indic-script OCR if present.
The 6-week roadmap must explicitly include data collection. The project will stall if annotated documents and
reference templates are assumed to exist.
At least 2-3 template versions if available.
:100-300 documents after MVP baseline is functional.
Rule 1 - Do not over-label entire sentences
This is to certify that A. Ravi Kumar has completed the course..
Only A. Ravi Kumar should be OPEN_VARIABLE. The rest is CONSTANT..
Rule 2 - Controlled vocabulary fields get CONTROLLED_VARIABLE
Rule 3 - Tables must be annotated cell-wise
P1 should use a shared document intelligence core so that P2 can reuse it later..
Quality score should be logged as a calibration feature, not just a rejection condition..
PaddleOCR is a strong practical default because its ecosystem includes OCR and document-structure modules,.
including table-structure recognition through PP-Structure / table recognition components. The official PaddleOCR
documentation describes table structure recognition as converting non-editable table images into editable formats.
such as HTML by identifying rows, columns, and cells.1.
Indian university documents may contain Telugu, Hindi, Sanskrit, or other Indic text in seals, headings, and signa.
tures. The system should not assume English-only OCR is sufficient..
The baseline is the first system to build.
Normalize all OcR boxes to page size
Store text, bbox, font/visual cues, OCR confidence.
GRoup OCR boxes across documents by approximate position.
ELSE IF value belongs to controlled lookup list:.
weak labels for a later token classifier,
Marksheets are table-heavy, so they need a dedicated table path
Use PaddleOCR PP-Structure / table structure recognition as the first default because it fits naturally with the OCR
stack and avoids introducing a separate table-recognition framework mid-sprint. Its table recognition documentation
describes row, column, and cell-position recognition for converting table images into structured formats such as
.Fine-tuned table detector if enough marksheets exist.
-> classify cells as CONTROLLED_VARIABLE or OPEN_VARIABLE
-> validate subject codes and grade patterns
Do not train many models in parallel at the beginning..
3. One token/layout classifier if enough annotation exists.
5. Optional visual detector for seal/logo/signature only if needed..
.Donut-style OCR-free model only after OCR baseline is understood..
This is miscalibrated and produces misleading confidence values..
Was this extracted field actually correct after human review?
12. Evaluation Plan and Numeric MVP Targets.
The headline metric should be template-holdout performance, not random split performance.
OCR character error rate on clean English text.
Every output must include a schema version.
Template drift means the institution quietly changes the layout, logo, seal placement, font, or wording
route sample documents to admin review
The privacy design should explicitly align with India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, which provides a
framework for processing digital personal data while recognizing individuals' right to protect their personal data.3
. Consent or valid processing basis where required.
. Encryption at rest and in transit.
:No unnecessary long-term storage of raw student documents.
.Build upload, preprocessing, quality scoring, and OCR pipeline.
.Store OCR boxes in a common schema.
. OCR boxes and quality scores are generated for all documents.
.Build lookup lists for degree, branch, semester, regulation, subject code.
:System extracts 10-15 key fields from degree certificates..
.Output includes interpretable labels and confidence components.
.At least one marksheet template produces structured subject-wise JSON..
. Table cell extraction F1 reaches at least 0.75 on the annotated subset..
Week 4 - Token/Layout Model Optional MVP Extension.
. Train or fine-tune one token/layout classifier if annotation volume is sufficient..
.Keep baseline if model does not improve template-holdout score.
. System chooses baseline or model based on validation performance, not assumption.
. Build review queue for low-confidence fields.
. Add error taxonomy and structured logs.
. Demo shows certificate extraction and marksheet table extraction.
P1 is a privacy-aware academic document extraction system that classifies document content into CONSTANT,
CONTROLLED_VARIABLE, and OPEN_VARIABLE regions, extracts structured fields from degree certificates
and marksheets, reconstructs marksheet tables using a dedicated table-structure module, calibrates confidence using
validation data, and reports template-holdout performance as the main success metric..
